fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes killed IRGC intelligence chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi on Monday in an intelligence-led airstrike.
Israeli strikes on Tuesday targeted IRGC sites, missile sites, and petrochemical plants including Iran's largest facility in Eslamiyeh and South Pars in Asaluyeh.
Israeli Defense Minister called strikes on petrochemical facilities a 'severe economic blow' worth tens of billions.
IRGC vowed 'Operation Crushing Revenge' against Israel for Khademi's killing via official Sepah News statement.
Iran launched 14 missiles and 19 drones at UAE, with all intercepted and 4 wounded reported.
Iran launched 7 ballistic missiles at Saudi Arabia, all downed.
Iranian missiles killed 4 people in Israel.
UAE reports 519 ballistic missiles, 26 cruise missiles, and 2,210 drones intercepted since war start, with 221 wounded and 13 killed.
At least 49 civilians were killed and 58 injured in Iran during Monday strikes, according to HRANA.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in late February strikes; his son Mojtaba Khamenei now serves as successor.
Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement that adversaries' security must be removed.
President Masoud Pezeshkian accused IRGC commanders of unilateral actions wrecking ceasefire chances and pushing Iran toward disaster.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy, was appointed Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing killed Ali Larijani.
Hardliners labeled ex-Foreign Minister Zarif a 'traitor' for calling to end the war, urging his and ex-President Rouhani's arrest.
President Trump threatened to strike Iranian power plants, bridges, and civilian infrastructure if no ceasefire deal is reached by Tuesday night.
Trump noted Iran is negotiating 'in good faith' but emphasized free oil traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.
Pakistan proposed a two-phase ceasefire plan outlining ending hostilities, reopening the Strait of Hormuz, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran received a 15-point US plan via Pakistan and is reviewing it, though Tehran denied intent to negotiate.
Trump reported productive US-Iran talks via envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff with a top Iranian official, postponing attacks on energy sites for five days.
Iran's parliament speaker denied negotiations with the US occurred.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran is reviewing the US plan.
A senior Iranian official insisted the war ends only on Iran's terms, including cessation of all-front fighting and attack guarantees.
Iran rejected a prior US ceasefire offer on April 6.
War disrupts Iran's Dubai trade lifeline and shadow financial networks; UAE arrests of IRGC-linked money changers may expand.
An 18-year-old protester Amirhossein Hatami was executed; authorities refuse to return his body four days post-execution.
A fourth protester was executed Saturday over a Basij base fire, with at least five executions in the past week linked to protests.
Israeli strikes hit repression infrastructure including intelligence compounds, police stations, Basij bases, and judicial buildings.
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and armed forces spokesperson warned of global attacks on adversaries, including tourist centers.
Over 2,000 additional US Marines are due in the Middle East by Friday during the five-day pause.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 42 escalation intensifies with Israeli strikes on April 7-8 killing IRGC intelligence chief Khademi and targeting petrochemical infrastructure (economic damage worth tens of billions), while Iran launches regional attacks and vows 'Operation Crushing Revenge.' Internal regime fractures widen as hardliners consolidate power under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, President Pezeshkian publicly accuses IRGC of sabotaging ceasefires, and authorities execute protesters at accelerating pace, while contradictory diplomatic signals persist with Trump claiming good-faith negotiations and a 15-point U.S. peace plan under review, yet Iranian leadership publicly denying talks.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli strikes on April 7 killed IRGC intelligence chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi and targeted Iran's largest petrochemical facilities in Eslamiyeh and South Pars, causing tens of billions in economic damage while triggering Iranian regional attacks and 'Operation Crushing Revenge' vows.
